definitions.definition: The Indus Valley Civilization was an urbanized civilization that flourished in the Indus Valley region in present-day Pakistan and northwestern India around 4300-1300 BCE. It is known for its sophisticated urban planning, architecture, and water management systems.
definitions.example: Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa were two of the major cities of the Indus Valley Civilization.

definitions.definition: Mohenjo-Daro was a major city of the Indus Valley Civilization located in the Sindh province of Pakistan. It is known for its sophisticated urban planning, architecture, and water management systems.
definitions.example: Mohenjo-Daro was an important center of trade and commerce during the Indus Valley Civilization.

definitions.definition: Harappa was a major city of the Indus Valley Civilization located in the Punjab province of Pakistan. It is known for its sophisticated urban planning, architecture, and water management systems.
definitions.example: Harappa was an important center of trade and commerce during the Indus Valley Civilization.

definitions.definition: The Chalcolithic period refers to the transition from the Copper Age to the Bronze Age, characterized by the use of copper and bronze tools and the development of complex societies.
definitions.example: The Indus Valley Civilization was a Chalcolithic society.

definitions.definition: Urbanization refers to the process of people moving from rural areas to cities, leading to the growth of urban centers and the development of complex societies.
definitions.example: The Indus Valley Civilization was one of the earliest examples of urbanization in the Indian subcontinent.

definitions.definition: The Copper Age refers to the period in human history when copper was first used as a material for tools and other objects.
definitions.example: The Copper Age preceded the Bronze Age and the Chalcolithic period.

definitions.definition: The Bronze Age refers to the period in human history when bronze was first used as a material for tools and other objects.
definitions.example: The Bronze Age followed the Copper Age and the Chalcolithic period.

definitions.definition: Agriculture refers to the practice of cultivating crops and rearing animals for food and other products.
definitions.example: Agriculture played a crucial role in the development of complex societies in the Indus Valley Civilization.

definitions.definition: Trade refers to the exchange of goods and services between different regions or communities.
definitions.example: Trade played a crucial role in the development of the Indus Valley Civilization.

definitions.definition: Specialization refers to the process of people developing specific skills or expertise in a particular area.
definitions.example: Specialization led to the growth of trade and commerce in the Indus Valley Civilization.

definitions.definition: Guilds refer to associations of craftsmen or traders who worked together to promote their interests and protect their rights.
definitions.example: Guilds played a crucial role in the development of trade and commerce in the Indus Valley Civilization.

definitions.definition: Monarchy refers to a system of government in which a king or queen holds supreme power and authority.
definitions.example: The Indus Valley Civilization was a monarchical society.

definitions.definition: Priesthood refers to the role of priests or priestesses who performed rituals and ceremonies on behalf of the community.
definitions.example: The Indus Valley Civilization had a well-developed priesthood.

definitions.definition: Craftsmen refer to skilled workers who created goods such as pottery, weaving, and metalwork.
definitions.example: Craftsmen played a crucial role in the development of the Indus Valley Civilization.

definitions.definition: Craft specialization refers to the process of craftsmen developing specific skills or expertise in a particular area.
definitions.example: Craft specialization led to the growth of trade and commerce in the Indus Valley Civilization.

definitions.definition: Urban centers refer to cities or towns that were the hubs of trade, commerce, and culture.
definitions.example: The Indus Valley Civilization had several urban centers, including Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa.

definitions.definition: Rural economy refers to the economy of rural areas, which was based on agriculture and other forms of subsistence production.
definitions.example: The rural economy played a crucial role in the development of the Indus Valley Civilization.

definitions.definition: Subsistence production refers to the production of goods and services for personal consumption rather than for trade or exchange.
definitions.example: Subsistence production was an important feature of the rural economy in the Indus Valley Civilization.
